title: woodsia cystopteroides
intro: Woodsia cystopteroides, also known as the alpine woodsia or spleenwort, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Woodsia cystopteroides include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Woodsia cystopteroides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
